Title: Cellulose synthase-like D movement in the plasma membrane requires enzymatic activity

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like D family glycosyltransferase; GT2; conserved D,D,D,QxxRW catalytic motifs; transmembrane protein
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with CSLD6 to support normal phyllid development and cell adhesion/patterning; loss together with CSLD6 causes defective gametophore morphology
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Enriched in leafy gametophores and sporophytes relative to protonemata
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall at tip-growing cell apex and developing cell plate
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasmic puncta; apical plasma membrane of protonemal tips; developing cell plate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLD2 is one of the gametophore-enriched moss CSLDs. 2. Single csld2 knockout shows little phenotype, but combined loss with CSLD6 causes severe phyllid patterning, adhesion, and expansion defects. 3. Native-promoter CSLD2 expression rescues csld2/6KO morphology, supporting redundant β-1,4-glucan synthase function in gametophore development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene function inferred from mutant/rescue phenotypes and family assignment; direct motility/catalytic assays were not performed specifically for CSLD2.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan in the cell wall/plasma membrane system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss together with CSLD6 causes abnormal phyllid morphology and cell separation defects

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like D family glycosyltransferase; GT2; conserved D,D,D,QxxRW catalytic motifs; transmembrane protein
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synthesizes β-1,4-glucan required for tip growth integrity, normal cell plate stabilization, and redundant gametophore patterning; catalytic activity is required for plasma membrane movement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Enriched in leafy gametophores; lower/less detectable transcript abundance than CSLD2 in some datasets
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in gametophores and protonemata; accumulates strongly in growing protonemal tips, branches, and developing cell plates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Nascent cell wall at apical plasma membrane and developing cell plate
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasmic puncta; apical plasma membrane; phragmoplast midzone/developing cell plate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endogenous fluorescent tagging; live-cell confocal imaging; VAEM particle tracking; catalytic point mutant (D867N/TEN); knockout and complementation; inhibitor assays with DCB/isoxaben; cell division imaging
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLD6 localizes to cytoplasmic puncta, the apical plasma membrane of tip-growing cells, and early developing cell plates. 2. CSLD6 particles move in linear plasma-membrane trajectories, and this movement requires catalytic activity because the D867N TEN mutant is immotile. 3. DCB, but not isoxaben, inhibits CSLD6 movement and reveals CSLD-specific roles in tip integrity and nascent cell plate stabilization. 4. CSLD6 acts redundantly with CSLD2 in gametophore phyllid development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CSLD6 trajectories are faster, shorter, and less linear than CESA10 and are largely independent of cortical microtubules, supporting distinct complexes from CESA. Arrival at cell tips depends on actin, but recruitment to the cell plate does not.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate cell wall assembly during tip growth/cytokinesis, but perturbation risks severe developmental defects and cell wall instability
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes β-1,4-glucan chains at the plasma membrane/cell plate; polymer production likely drives enzyme complex movement in the membrane
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for tip wall integrity and early cell plate stabilization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Actin depolymerization by latrunculin B; DCB inhibits movement/activity; isoxaben has no effect on CSLD6 movement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytic inactivation or combined loss with CSLD2 causes phyllid patterning defects; DCB inhibition causes tip rupture and cell plate deformation

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase family glycosyltransferase; GT2; transmembrane cellulose synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as a comparison enzyme showing slower, straighter, isoxaben-sensitive plasma membrane trajectories distinct from CSLD6
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall/plasma membrane-associated cellulose deposition sites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endogenous mEGFP tagging; VAEM imaging; particle tracking; inhibitor response comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA10 moves in linear plasma membrane trajectories typical of cellulose synthase complexes. 2. CESA10 particles are slower and more linear than CSLD6 particles. 3. Used as a comparator demonstrating functional distinction between CESA and CSLD complexes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary subject of mechanistic dissection for the study’s main conclusion, but experimentally analyzed in Results as direct comparator.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes cellulose β-1,4-glucan chains assembled into microfibrils
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Isoxaben sensitive based on comparative statement
